overview.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. operates as a bank and financial holding company in the United States, rest of North America, Europe, the Middle East, Africa, the Asia Pacific, Latin America, and the Caribbean. It operates in three segments: Consumer & Community Banking, Commercial & Investment Bank, and Asset & Wealth Management. The company offers deposit, investment and lending products, and cash management; mortgage origination and servicing activities; residential mortgages and home equity loans; and credit cards, payment solutions, travel services, ...
overview.financialIntro: In 2025, JPMorgan Chase's revenue was $168.24 billion, an increase of 0.88% compared to the previous year's $166.78 billion. Earnings were $55.68 billion, a decrease of -2.09%.
overview.analystIntro: According to 24 analysts, the average rating for JPM stock is "Buy." The 12-month stock price target is $342.9, which is an increase of 2.32% from the latest price.

data.title: The Options Trader Who Emerged as a Surprise Front-Runner to Succeed Jamie Dimon
data.text: Troy Rohrbaugh is seen as the leading contender in a race with Doug Petno to run JPMorgan.

data.title: How JPMorgan went from 3 female CEO contenders to an all-male succession race
data.text: Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh became front-runners in JPMorgan's succession race Thursday. Less than two years ago, three women had been among the top contenders for Jamie Dimon's job.

data.title: The Long Race to Succeed Jamie Dimon Heats Up
data.text: JPMorgan Chase promoted Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh to co-presidents. In addition, Marianne Lake, a longtime lieutenant to Chairman and Chief Executive Jamie Dimon, is retiring

data.title: JPMorgan's executive reshuffle spotlights Dimon succession plan
data.text: JPMorgan Chase JPM.N on Thursday named insiders Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh as the bank's co-presidents, in the latest move to pave the way for longtime CEO Jamie Dimon's eventual succession.
